title: Puerto Banus Boat Rental 2026: Costs, Types and Where to Start
html:
Boat rental in Puerto Banus offers a gateway to the Marbella coastline, with half-day motorboat charters starting around 450 EUR. Whether you are a first-timer or a seasoned mariner, BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own, giving access to verified options from local operators.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Prices in 2026 range from 50 EUR for a shared tour to over 3,000 EUR for a full-day crewed yacht. The final figure depends on boat size, season, and extras such as fuel and skipper.

  • Shared tour (2-3 hours) from 50 EUR per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, no licence needed guide) from 450 EUR
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, up to 12 guests) from 1,200 EUR
  • Crewed yacht full-day (up to 12 guests, with skipper and host) from 2,500 EUR

To see a full range of vessels, you can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 for transparent pricing and availability at compare verified boat listings.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

The main departure point is Puerto Banus itself, a marina lined with restaurants and designer boutiques. Nearby options include Marbella’s Puerto Deportivo, San Pedro de Alcántara’s small port, and Estepona’s marina to the west. For those staying inland, you can launch from the beach clubs at Rio Verde or Torreblanca. From Banus, the open sea lies just beyond the breakwater, with the Sierra Blanca mountains as a backdrop.

Which boat to rent

  1. Azimut 58, best for luxury day trips with up to 12 guests, from 2,800 EUR per day; see the Azimut 58 for hire.
  2. Sunseeker Predator 50, ideal for a stylish couple’s outing or group of 8, from 1,800 EUR per half-day.
  3. Fairline Phantom 48, a spacious family cruiser for up to 10, from 1,200 EUR per half-day.
  4. Axopar 37, a versatile day boat for up to 10, perfect for exploring coves, from 700 EUR per half-day.
  5. Lagoon 40 catamaran, stable and relaxed for 12 guests, from 1,600 EUR per day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

No licence is required for charters with a skipper, which most rental companies in Spain insist on for powerboats over 15 metres. For smaller boats without a skipper, a licence (such as the Spanish PER) is normally needed. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking. The high season runs from June to September, when the Poniente wind occasionally picks up in the afternoon, making morning trips calmer.

Book a morning departure to avoid the afternoon Poniente wind, which can make the sea choppy around the Benabola area.

At a glance

  • Price band: 50, 3,000+ EUR
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full-day
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on most private charters
  • Currency: EUR

When booking, use the boat search on BoatHire24 to filter by date, capacity and price, and see genuine availability.

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a boat in Puerto Banus for a photoshoot?

Yes, many yachts in Puerto Banus are available specifically for photoshoots, with hourly rates from around 400 EUR.

What is the best time of day to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

Morning departures between 9am and noon offer the calmest seas and best light.

Is it possible to visit Marbella from Puerto Banus by boat?

The cruise takes about 30 minutes each way, covering roughly five nautical miles along the Costa del Sol.

Can I bring my own food and drink on a rental boat?

Most charter companies allow you to bring your own provisions, though catered options are often available for an additional fee.

For 2026, BoatHire24 continues to connect renters with reliable boat owners in Puerto Banus. If you own a vessel, consider listing it on the platform to reach thousands of travellers each season. You can rent out your boat through BoatHire24.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
